name: Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)
author: OpenTikZ contributors
description: Block-level GAN overview: a low-dimensional latent bar feeds the generator G (expanding trapezoid) producing generated fake images, while a high-dimensional sample-space stack supplies real images; both feed the discriminator D (compressing trapezoid) that outputs a real/fake decision. Parametric sizes, colors, and labels.

parameters.name: \trapW
parameters.meaning: horizontal length of the G and D trapezoids

parameters.name: \trapBig
parameters.meaning: long (wide) parallel edge of each trapezoid

parameters.name: \trapSmall
parameters.meaning: short (narrow) parallel edge of each trapezoid

parameters.name: \imgW
parameters.meaning: width of the Real/Fake image boxes

parameters.name: \imgH
parameters.meaning: height of the Real/Fake image boxes

parameters.name: \rowsep
parameters.meaning: vertical gap between the top (real) and bottom (fake) rows; D and the output sit on the midline

parameters.name: \colgap
parameters.meaning: horizontal gap between columns; also the flow-arrow length

parameters.name: \latentW
parameters.meaning: width of the latent-space bar

parameters.name: \cellH
parameters.meaning: height of one latent-space cell

parameters.name: \latentcells
parameters.meaning: number of latent cells; keep equal to the length of \latentcolors (it drives the bar's vertical centering)
parameters.invariant: must equal the number of entries in \latentcolors

parameters.name: \stackS
parameters.meaning: side length of each sample-space plane

parameters.name: \stackoff
parameters.meaning: diagonal offset between stacked sample planes

parameters.name: \stackcount
parameters.meaning: number of sample planes; keep equal to the length of \stackcolors
parameters.invariant: must equal the number of entries in \stackcolors

parameters.name: \outR
parameters.meaning: radius of the Real/Fake output lights

parameters.name: \latentcolors
parameters.meaning: per-cell palette tints of the latent bar (bottom-to-top); one entry per cell
parameters.default: otblue!45, otteal!40, otgray!25, otorange!45, otblue!28, otpurple!40, otorange!30, otgray!40

parameters.name: \stackcolors
parameters.meaning: per-plane palette tints of the sample-space stack (back-to-front)

parameters.name: \latentlabel
parameters.meaning: label left of the latent bar (\\ for line breaks)

parameters.name: \samplelabel
parameters.meaning: label left of the sample-space stack (\\ for line breaks)

parameters.name: \genlabel
parameters.meaning: generator name drawn inside G (above the symbol)

parameters.name: \gensym
parameters.meaning: generator symbol drawn inside G, e.g. $G$

parameters.name: \disclabel
parameters.meaning: discriminator name drawn inside D (above the symbol)

parameters.name: \discsym
parameters.meaning: discriminator symbol drawn inside D, e.g. $D$

parameters.name: \reallabel
parameters.meaning: label inside the real-images box

parameters.name: \fakelabel
parameters.meaning: label inside the generated-fake-images box

parameters.name: \realout
parameters.meaning: label above the top (real) output light

parameters.name: \fakeout
parameters.meaning: label below the bottom (fake) output light

edit_contract.node_naming: fixed semantic node names: (gen) (disc) are the centre text nodes of the G/D trapezoids; (real) (fake) are the image boxes; (realout) (fakeout) are the output lights. The latent bar and sample stack are drawn as \foreach groups (no single node); arrows attach to the computed edges \latentRight and \stackRight.

operations.summary: edit the matching label macro (\genlabel, \disclabel, \reallabel, \fakelabel, \latentlabel, \samplelabel, \realout, \fakeout); no layout change needed
change the palette name in the G \filldraw (otblue), the D \filldraw (otpurple), the imgbox style (otteal), or the output-light fills (otteal/otorange); never a hex/stock color
\trapW/\trapBig/\trapSmall for the trapezoids, \imgW/\imgH for the image boxes, \rowsep for row spacing, \colgap for column spacing/arrow length
set the number/colors of latent cells via \latentcolors (and matching \latentcells), and sample planes via \stackcolors (and matching \stackcount)
add a callout near (disc) or the output describing the adversarial objective; use a palette-colored lbl node and keep existing nodes intact (see reference/annotations/)

invariants: keep G expanding (narrow left, wide right) and D compressing (wide left, narrow right) so the generator/discriminator roles stay readable
keep \latentcells equal to the length of \latentcolors and \stackcount equal to the length of \stackcolors
colors only via the five palette names (tints/shades allowed); never inline hex or stock colors
